description: Aging biology, oxidative stress, telomere maintenance, and detox pathways

rs4880.name: A16V

CC.interpretation: Homozygous - lower mitochondrial antioxidant capacity This is a statistical association that interacts with lifestyle, environment, and other genetic factors.
recommendations: Prioritize antioxidant-rich diet
Consider NAC supplementation
Avoid high-dose iron

CT.interpretation: Heterozygous variant - lower mitochondrial targeting This is a statistical association that interacts with lifestyle, environment, and other genetic factors.
recommendations: Dietary antioxidants especially important

TT.interpretation: For a16v, your SOD2 variant is one contributing factor — good mitochondrial antioxidant protection
recommendations: Standard antioxidant needs

A/A.interpretation: A16V — your SOD2 genotype suggests a favorable profile that may confer some natural advantage. This is a statistical association from population studies, not a guarantee of any outcome.
recommendations: Continue healthy lifestyle habits that support a16v
Genotype alone does not override environmental and behavioral factors
Ensure adequate dietary antioxidants
Consider selenium supplementation

bibliography: Ahmetov II, Naumov VA, Donnikov AE, Maciejewska-Kar?owska A, Kostryukova ES, Larin AK, et al. SOD2
gene polymorphism and muscle damage markers in elite athletes. Free Radic Res, 2014; 48(8):94855.
Akimoto AK, Miranda-Vilela AL, Alves PCZ, Pereira LC da S, Lordelo GS, Hiragi C de O, et al. Evaluation
of gene polymorphisms in exercise-induced oxidative stress and damage. Free Radic Res, 2010;
Baumert P, Lake MJ, Stewart CE, Drust B, Erskine RM. Genetic variation and exercise-induced
muscle damage: implications for athletic performance, injury and ageing. Eur J Appl Physiol, 2016;
Sprouse C, Gordish-Dressman H, Orkunoglu-Suer EF, Lipof JS, Moeckel-Cole S, Patel RR, et al. SLC30A8
Nonsynonymous Variant Is Associated With Recovery Following Exercise and Skeletal Muscle Size and
Strength. Diabetes, 2014; 63(1):3638.
rs4880.technical_report:
As we have indicated, in order for muscle regeneration to occur after exercise, there must be
an inflammatory signal through which the satellite cells are activated. There are studies that

 relate certain genetic variants with this inflammatory signal and, therefore, with a greater or
lesser capacity for muscle remodeling. A lower capacity for muscle remodeling implies that
a longer recovery is required after an injury. Two genetic polymorphisms have been
identified that are related to this inflammatory signal and therefore to a greater or lesser
capacity for muscle regeneration. These polymorphisms are found in the SOD2 and
SLC30A8 genes. The SOD2 gene produces the superoxide dismutase 2 protein, which has antioxidant activity,
protecting the muscle from free radicals and facilitating muscle regeneration. The lack of
functionality of this protein can cause the accumulation of free radicals and cell death of
muscle fibers. On the other hand, the SLC30A8 gene is associated with insulin response, and is indirectly
associated with muscle strength and mass, as glycogen synthesis and glucose transport to
the muscle are affected.
rs4880.description:
PDF

Exercise-induced muscle damage (regeneration capacity)
Variants affecting genes involved in the activation of the cells responsible for muscle
regeneration are relevant to understanding why some people require more time to recover
after a muscle injury.

rs1050450.name: Selenium Utilization

TT.interpretation: For selenium utilization, your GPX1 variant is one contributing factor — the gpx1 pro198leu variant reduces glutathione peroxidase activity — a key selenium-dependent antioxidant enzyme This is a statistical association that interacts with lifestyle, environment, and other genetic factors.
recommendations: Ensure adequate selenium intake (Brazil nuts are the richest source — 1-2/day provides RDA)
Selenium supplementation (selenomethionine 100-200mcg/day) if dietary intake is low
Adequate glutathione precursors: N-acetylcysteine (NAC), glycine, glutamine
Selenium status can be Tracked via serum selenium or GPX activity

CT.interpretation: For selenium utilization, your GPX1 variant is one contributing factor — intermediate glutathione peroxidase activity
recommendations: Ensure adequate selenium intake (55mcg/day minimum)
Brazil nuts, sardines, eggs, sunflower seeds are excellent sources

CC.interpretation: For selenium utilization, your GPX1 variant is one contributing factor — normal glutathione peroxidase function
recommendations: Maintain adequate selenium intake
Selenium is also important for thyroid hormone conversion (deiodinase enzymes)

G/A.interpretation: Your GPX1 genotype is one of several genetic factors linked to selenium utilization. Research in this area continues to evolve, and most traits have multiple genetic and environmental contributors.
recommendations: Stay informed as new research clarifies the role of GPX1 in selenium utilization
Consider discussing with a healthcare provider familiar with genetic interpretation

bibliography: Chen J., Cao Q., et al. GPx-1 polymorphism (rs1050450) contributes to tumor susceptibility: evidence from
meta-analysis. J Cancer Res Clin Oncol. 2011 Oct;137(10):1553-61.
Bastaki M., Huen K., et al. Genotype-activity relationship for Mn-superoxide dismutase, glutathione
peroxidase 1 and catalase in humans. Pharmacogenet Genomics. 2006 Apr;16(4):279-86.
Liu K., Liu X., et al. Two common functional catalase gene polymorphisms (rs1001179 and rs794316) and
cancer susceptibility: evidence from 14,942 cancer cases and 43,285 controls. Oncotarget. 2016 Sep
Minlikeeva A.N., Browne R.W., et al. Single-Nucleotide Polymorphisms and Markers of Oxidative Stress in
Healthy Women. PLoS One. 2016 Jun 7;11(6):e0156450.
rs1050450.technical_report:
The activity of enzymes responsible for combating oxidative damage from reactive oxygen
species (ROSS) can be affected by factors such as diet, drug intake or polymorphisms
affecting genes involved in antioxidant function. Several studies have identified some
mutations that affect the activity of antioxidant enzymes. These mutations have been
identified in genes such as the CAT gene and the GPX1 gene. The CAT gene codes for the enzyme catalase, which mediates the control of the
most important endogenous enzymatic antioxidants. It catalyzes the decomposition of 2
molecules of hydrogen peroxide into 1 molecule of oxygen and 2 molecules of water, and is
therefore responsible for hydrogen peroxide concentrations. In turn, the GPX1 gene produces the enzyme glutathione peroxidase, which is a seleniumdependent enzyme. This enzyme is important because it mediates protecting red
blood cells from hemoglobin by reducing peroxides and lipoperoxides, using glutathione as
a substrate. Disruption of GPx-1 activity leads to increased levels of peroxides and
lipoperoxides (inducers of oxidative stress). Its affinity for oxygen peroxide is higher than
that of the enzyme catalase, making the enzyme glutathione peroxidase the main
antioxidant of oxygen peroxides. Reduced antioxidant enzyme activity can lead to the accumulation of reactive oxygen
species and promote the development of diseases such as obesity, encephalopathies, renal
failure, diabetes, cervical cancer and prostate cancer.
rs1050450.description:
Antioxidant capacity
The body's antioxidant defense system, especially the enzymes glutathione peroxidase and
catalase, are responsible for keeping the levels of reactive oxygen species (ROS) low to
avoid a physiological state of oxidative stress.

rs1001179.name: P200L

CC.interpretation: For p200l, your GPX1 variant is one contributing factor — standard glutathione peroxidase function
recommendations: Standard selenium intake adequate

CT.interpretation: For p200l, your GPX1 variant is one contributing factor — heterozygous variant may reduce gpx activity This is a statistical association that interacts with lifestyle, environment, and other genetic factors.
recommendations: Ensure adequate selenium
Brazil nuts

TT.interpretation: Homozygous variant - altered hydrogen peroxide removal This is a statistical association that interacts with lifestyle, environment, and other genetic factors.
recommendations: Selenium supplementation
Prioritize GPX-supportive antioxidants

C/C.interpretation: Your GPX1 genotype is one of several genetic factors linked to p200l. Research in this area continues to evolve, and most traits have multiple genetic and environmental contributors.
recommendations: Stay informed as new research clarifies the role of GPX1 in p200l
Consider discussing with a healthcare provider familiar with genetic interpretation

rs6025.name: Blood coagulation, factor V Leiden and 20210G-A
rs6025.display: F5 - Blood coagulation, factor V Leiden and 20210G-A

C/C.interpretation: Your F5 genotype is the most common form in reference populations. This does not rule out blood coagulation, factor v leiden and 20210g-a risk from other genes, environmental exposures, or rare variants not covered by standard genotyping.
recommendations: No specific F5-directed intervention indicated, but standard health screening still applies
Other genetic and environmental factors contribute to blood coagulation, factor v leiden and 20210g-a — genotype alone is not the full picture

GG.interpretation: For blood coagulation, factor v leiden and 20210g-a, your F5 variant is one contributing factor — no factor v leiden variant - normal coagulation
recommendations: Standard thrombotic risk

GA.interpretation: Heterozygous - moderately elevated clotting risk (4-8x) This is a statistical association that interacts with lifestyle, environment, and other genetic factors.
recommendations: Avoid estrogen-containing contraceptives
Consider thrombophilia testing before surgery
Genetic counseling

AA.interpretation: notably elevated clotting risk (40-80x) This is a statistical association that interacts with lifestyle, environment, and other genetic factors.
recommendations: Priority hematology Discuss with
Avoid high-risk activities
Special precautions for surgery/pregnancy

bibliography: Bertina RM, Koeleman BP, Koster T, Rosendaal FR, Dirven RJ, de Ronde H, van der Velden PA, Reitsma PH.
Mutation in blood coagulation factor V associated with resistance to activated protein C. Nature.
Bertina RM, Koeleman BP, Koster T, Rosendaal FR, Dirven RJ, de Ronde H, van der Velden PA, Reitsma PH.
Mutation in blood coagulation factor V associated with resistance to activated protein C. Nature.
Doherty TM, Kelley A. Bleeding Disorders. 2019 Jun 16. StatPearls [Internet]. Treasure Island (FL):
StatPearls Publishing; 2020 Jan-.
Kujovich JL. Factor V Leiden thrombophilia. Genet Med. 2011;13(1):1-16.
Simone B, De Stefano V, Leoncini E, Zacho J, Martinelli I, Emmerich J, et al. Risk of venous
thromboembolism associated with single and combined effects of Factor V Leiden, Prothrombin 20210A
and Methylenetethraydrofolate reductase C677T: a meta-analysis involving over 11,000 cases and 21,000
controls. Eur J Epidemiol. 2013;28(8):621-47.
Stevens SM, Woller SC, Bauer KA, Kasthuri R, Cushman M, Streiff M, et al. Guidance for the evaluation and
treatment of hereditary and acquired thrombophilia. J Thromb Thrombolysis. 2016;41(1):154-64.
rs6025.technical_report:
The risk of blood hypercoagulation is largely affected by the genetic component. It is known
that the presence of mutations such as factor V Leiden or the prothrombin gene mutation
20210G-A are associated with the risk of hereditary thrombophilias. The Factor V Leiden mutation occurs in the F5 gene and is more frequent in Caucasian
populations than in those of other origins, so it is estimated that between 3 and 8% of the
European or US Caucasian population carries at least one copy of the mutation. When the Factor V Leiden mutation occurs in the F5 gene, the mutated Factor V Leiden
becomes more resistant to the action of APC (activated protein C with inactivating effect of
Factor V Leiden), which promotes the generation of thrombin, an enzyme that promotes
blood clotting. The presence of the factor V Leiden mutation in the F5 gene has been found to be one of the
main genetic factors leading to venous thromboembolism (VTE), a disorder that includes
deep vein thromboembolism and pulmonary embolisms. VTE is characterized by the
abnormal formation of blood clots, which in this case mainly affect the veins of the legs. On the other hand, the 20210G-A marker has been identified in the F2 gene, which produces
the prothrombin protein, a glycoprotein formed in the liver that plays a vital role in the
formation of blood clots.The frequency with which this glycoprotein is produced in the liver
plays a vital role in the conversion of fibrinogen to fibrin during clot formation, in order to

 stop bleeding when an injury occurs. The frequency with which this mutation occurs is low,
being more common in the population of European descent than in other populations.
rs6025.description:
PDF

Blood coagulation, factor V Leiden and 20210G-A
Thrombophilias are hereditary and/or acquired pathologies that predispose to blood clots
or thrombosis. Among the genetic factors identified, the Factor V Leiden mutation and the
prothrombin gene 20210G-A mutation are the most studied.

Discuss anticoagulation with physician
Genetic counseling

rs10195871.name: Persistence of fetal hemoglobin

G/G.interpretation: Your BCL11A genotype is one of several genetic factors linked to persistence of fetal hemoglobin. Research in this area continues to evolve, and most traits have multiple genetic and environmental contributors.
recommendations: Stay informed as new research clarifies the role of BCL11A in persistence of fetal hemoglobin
Consider discussing with a healthcare provider familiar with genetic interpretation

bibliography: Bauer DE, Orkin SH. Hemoglobin switching's surprise: the versatile transcription factor BCL11A is a master
repressor of fetal hemoglobin. Curr Opin Genet Dev. 2015 Aug;33:62-70.
Uda M, Galanello R, Sanna S, Lettre G, Sankaran VG, Chen W, et al. Genome-wide association study shows
BCL11A associated with persistent fetal hemoglobin and amelioration of the phenotype of betathalassemia. Proc Natl Acad Sci U S A [Internet]. 2008 Feb 5 [cited 2014 Sep 28];105(5):16205.
Liu N, Hargreaves VV, Zhu Q, Kurland JV, Hong J, Kim W, Sher F, et al. Direct Promoter Repression by
BCL11A Controls the Fetal to Adult Hemoglobin Switch. Cell. 2018 Apr 5;173(2):430-442.e17.
rs10195871.technical_report:
evidence suggests that the presence of fetal hemoglobin can reduce the severity of sickle
cell disease and beta thalassemia, so the detection of variants that facilitate the

 persistence of fetal hemoglobin in adults is of great interest for the development of
treatments against these diseases. The genome-wide association study carried out by Uda et al. showed that the BCL11A gene
is related to fetal hemoglobin production in adults. The BCL11A gene is a gene that is expressed in red blood cell precursors and gives rise to a
transcription factor that regulates the gamma-globin gene by inhibiting its expression.
rs10195871.description:
PDF

Persistence of fetal hemoglobin
It has been demonstrated that the persistence of fetal hemoglobin has a marked hereditary
character. There are several studies that relate certain genetic polymorphisms or variants
with the persistence of fetal hemoglobin in adults and its possible implication in the
improvement of symptoms of different types of anemia.

rs401681.name: PSA (Prostate Specific Antigen) Levels

C/C.interpretation: Your CLPTM1L genotype is one of several genetic factors linked to psa (prostate specific antigen) levels. Research in this area continues to evolve, and most traits have multiple genetic and environmental contributors.
recommendations: Stay informed as new research clarifies the role of CLPTM1L in psa (prostate specific antigen) levels
Consider discussing with a healthcare provider familiar with genetic interpretation

bibliography: Gudmundsson J, Besenbacher S, Sulem P, Gudbjartsson DF, Olafsson I, Arinbjarnarson S, et al.
Genetic correction of PSA values using sequence variants associated with PSA levels. Sci Transl
David MK et al. Prostate Specific Antigen. [May 2022]
Medscape. Prostate-Specific Antigen Testing [May 2022]
American Cancer Society. Prostate Cancer. Early detection, diagnosis, and staging [May 2022]
National Institute of Health. National Cancer Institute [May 2022]
rs401681.technical_report:
PSA levels can be affected for different reasons, either by a genetic or environmental factor. It is estimated that about 40% of the variability of PSA levels in the general population is
due to hereditary factors. Based on a genome-wide association study involving more than
15,000 men, 3 markers associated with variability in PSA levels have been identified. Among
the genes involved in this trait we can find the HNF1B gene or the MSMB gene, genes that
had been previously associated with the risk of colon cancer, as well as the CLPTM1L gene,
also previously associated with the development of different types of cancers.
rs401681.description:
PDF

PSA (Prostate Specific Antigen) Levels
The serum PSA value is useful for the follow-up and early detection of prostate cancer
since the probability of developing prostate cancer increases with higher values. However,
since there is no agreed cut-off point, additional tests are often required to establish the
diagnosis or to rule out other causes of its increase.

rs1047781.name: Secretor status and ABH antigens (FUT2 gene)
rs1047781.display: FUT2 - Secretor status and ABH antigens (FUT2 gene)

A/A.interpretation: Your FUT2 genotype is one of several genetic factors linked to secretor status and abh antigens (fut2 gene). Research in this area continues to evolve, and most traits have multiple genetic and environmental contributors.
recommendations: Stay informed as new research clarifies the role of FUT2 in secretor status and abh antigens (fut2 gene)
Consider discussing with a healthcare provider familiar with genetic interpretation

bibliography: Stowell CP, Stowell SR. Biologic roles of the ABH and Lewis histo-blood group antigens Part I: infection
and immunity. Vox Sang. 2019;114(5):426-442.
Stowell SR, Stowell CP. Biologic roles of the ABH and Lewis histo-blood group antigens part II: thrombosis,
cardiovascular disease and metabolism. Vox Sang. 2019 Aug;114(6):535-552.
Velkova A, Diaz JEL, Pangilinan F, Molloy AM, Mills JL, Shane B, Sanchez E, et al. The FUT2
secretor variant p.Trp154Ter influences serum vitamin B12 concentration via holo-haptocorrin,
but not holo-transcobalamin, and is associated with haptocorrin glycosylation. Hum Mol Genet.
Azad MB, Wade KH, Timpson NJ. FUT2 secretor genotype and susceptibility to infections and chronic
conditions in the ALSPAC cohort. Version 2. Wellcome Open Res. 2018.
rs1047781.technical_report:
Just as in determining a person's blood type, the ability to produce Lewis and ABH antigens
on red blood cells or other tissues is influenced by genetics. Two polymorphisms associated with this trait have been identified. One affects the
population of European ancestry (W143X), while the other affects the population of Asian
ancestry (I129F). Both polymorphisms are found in the FUT2 gene, affecting the enzymatic
activity α-1,2-fucosyltransferase, essential for the secretion of the soluble forms of the ABH
and Lewis blood group antigens in the mucosa and secretory glands of the intestine. Thus, carriers of the indicated mutations allow the population to be divided into two groups,
depending on their ABH antigen secretion status. The "non-secretory" phenotype is
characterized by not having these antigens in body secretions. It is believed that this can
lead to physiological changes in the tissue, affecting bacterial adherence and

 immunoglobulin status, so these changes have also been linked to the individual's
susceptibility to other pathogens and diseases, as well as to changes in vitamin
B12 metabolism.
rs1047781.description:
PDF

Secretor status and ABH antigens (FUT2 gene)
ABH blood group and Lewis antigens are not only found on red blood cells, where A and B
determine the group, but these molecules are also present in a wide variety of tissues and
can trigger susceptibility to infections and contribute to the development of other
thromboembolic and cardiovascular diseases.

GG.interpretation: Attenuated insulin/IGF-1 pathway — linked to longevity (caloric restriction mimicry), lower cancer and age-related disease risk in EPIC cohort analyses
recommendations: Low-glycemic diet to maintain low IGF-1 (complements genetic tendency)
Limit dairy (primary dietary driver of IGF-1 elevation)
Intermittent fasting — reduces circulating IGF-1 further
Resistance training to maintain lean mass (local IGF-1 signaling in muscle remains beneficial)

AG.interpretation: For a>g, your IGF1R variant is one contributing factor — one longevity allele — partial attenuation of igf-1 receptor activity
recommendations: Low-glycemic and intermittent fasting beneficial to complement genetic tendency

AA.interpretation: For a>g, your IGF1R variant is one contributing factor — standard igf-1 receptor activity — no inherited longevity pathway attenuation This is a statistical association that interacts with lifestyle, environment, and other genetic factors.
recommendations: Low-glycemic diet and periodic fasting especially impactful (no genetic head start on IGF-1 pathway)
Consider Tracking serum IGF-1 annually if concerned about cancer risk
